texto: A master in the art of living draws no sharp distinction between his work and his play; his labor and his leisure; his mind and his body; his education and his recreation. He hardly knows which is which. He simply pursues his vision of excellence through whatever he is doing, and leaves others to determine whether he is working or playing. To himself, he always appears to be doing both.
explicacao: Citação de Lawrence Pearsall Jacks sobre temas ligados a educacao. “A master in the art of living draws no sharp distinction between his work and his play; his labor…” articula educacao, leisure, play em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e play.
